input.description: Show details of B5 (refactor: ParkToProposed helper)

data.output:
ID:       ddx-94ff4f5d
Title:    refactor: extract Store.ParkToProposed helper to consolidate intake rejection
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:bead, area:lifecycle, kind:refactor, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-6e6d08f8
Desc:     PROBLEM
6 call sites in cli/internal/agent/{execute_bead_loop.go, execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_review_terminal.go, execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go} duplicate the pattern of parking a bead to status=proposed with an intake event.

ROOT CAUSE
parkBeadPostIntakeRejection exists in execute_bead_loop.go:4016-4050 and is called from 6+ locations, but it directly manipulates Store and appends events inline. The Store interface does not expose a single public method that encapsulates both the transition and the event atomically.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a new Store.ParkToProposed(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, at time.Time) error method that:
   - Calls TransitionLifecycle to set status=proposed with OperatorRequired=true.
   - Appends a structured intake event.
   - Returns any error.
2. Update parkBeadPostIntakeRejection to use the new Store.ParkToProposed (or deprecate parkBeadPostIntakeRejection entirely if it becomes redundant).
3. Replace all 6 agent call sites with the new Store.ParkToProposed method.
4. Verify no functional change: output beads must have identical status and events.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the intake rejection policy itself. Do not add new parameters or optional behavior.

Accept:   1. Store.ParkToProposed(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, at time.Time) error exists in store.go.
2. Store.ParkToProposed atomically transitions to proposed and appends an intake event.
3. grep -r 'parkBeadPostIntakeRejection' cli/internal/agent/*.go shows 0 references (all replaced).
4. All 6+ agent call sites (execute_bead_loop.go, execute_bead_post_review.go, etc.) use Store.ParkToProposed.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go passes (uses intake rejection).

input.description: Show details of B6 (refactor: consolidate claim-Extra key cleanup)

data.output:
ID:       ddx-4eb047cc
Title:    refactor: consolidate claim-Extra key cleanup from Reopen and Unclaim
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:bead, area:lifecycle, kind:refactor
Parent:   ddx-6e6d08f8
Desc:     PROBLEM
store.go:Reopen (lines 1664-1665) and store.go:Unclaim (lines 1065-1067) maintain parallel loops that delete the same set of claim metadata Extra keys. This duplication is error-prone: if ClaimMetadataExtraKeys is extended, both loops must be updated.

ROOT CAUSE
store.go:Reopen clears claim fields in isolation; store.go:Unclaim does the same separately. Both iterate ClaimMetadataExtraKeys but the operations are not factored into a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a private helper func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) that deletes all entries in ClaimMetadataExtraKeys from the Extra map.
2. Update store.go:Reopen:1664-1665 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
3. Update store.go:Unclaim:1064-1069 to call clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra).
4. Verify both methods also call RemoveClaimHeartbeat as before (Unclaim does; Reopen should also).

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the set of keys being cleared. Do not refactor heartbeat cleanup.

Accept:   1. clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) helper function exists in store.go.
2. store.go:Reopen calls clearClaimExtraKeys instead of inline loop.
3. store.go:Unclaim calls clearClaimExtraKeys instead of inline loop.
4. Both Reopen and Unclaim continue to remove claim heartbeats (RemoveClaimHeartbeat or direct Extra deletion).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/store_test.go::TestClaim passes (verifies Claim/Unclaim/Reopen workflow).
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/lifecycle_test.go passes (verifies claim transitions).

Updated:  2026-05-18 03:36:42
